panels.title: Overall Health
panels.description: Single-pane triage signal. HEALTHY = feed, workers and overlay fresh; DEGRADED = bridge stale/scrape error or workers not healthy; CRITICAL = feed down while market open or overlay stale.

panels.title: Incident Triage Guide
panels.description: Suggested first checks when Overall Health is not HEALTHY.

options.content:
**Incident triage quick-checks**

- `feed_healthy == 0` -> check `DATABENTO_API_KEY`, Railway logs, circuit breaker.
- `workers_healthy == 0` -> check thread liveness in Worker Liveness panel; restart via Railway.
- `overlay_fresh == 0` -> check compute cycle errors, full/flow compute logs.
- `market_open == 1` but no traffic -> check TradingView token rollout / auth denied rate.
- bridge scrape error -> verify UptimeRobot / GitHub API token and quota.
- vertical grey lines on time-series = recent deploy/restart.

Full runbook: [README](https://github.com/skippALGO/skipp-algo/blob/main/services/live_overlay_daemon/README.md)

panels.title: Feed Healthy
panels.description: Live feed connection health (0 = unhealthy, 1 = healthy).

panels.title: Overlay Fresh
panels.description: Overlay data freshness (1 = fresh, 0 = stale).

panels.title: Workers Healthy
panels.description: Background thread liveness (1 = all alive, 0 = at least one dead).

panels.title: Bridge Scrapes
panels.description: External bridge scrape health (UptimeRobot + GitHub Workflows).

panels.title: Market Status
panels.description: US regular trading session state (1 = open, 0 = closed).

panels.title: Last Bar Age
panels.description: Age of the newest bar received from Databento.

panels.title: Service Status
panels.description: Daemon lifecycle state: STARTING, HEALTHY, DEGRADED, CRITICAL or IDLE. Combines feed readiness, worker liveness, overlay freshness and market-open context.

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Daemon process uptime since the last start/restart.

panels.title: Overlay Symbols
panels.description: Number of distinct symbols currently held in the in-memory SMC overlay cache.

panels.title: Bar Symbols
panels.description: Number of distinct symbols with at least one 1-minute bar in the ingest cache.

panels.title: Bar Count
panels.description: Total 1-minute bars currently retained across all symbols.

panels.title: Request Rate
panels.description: HTTP request rate to the daemon endpoints as observed by Grafana Alloy.

panels.title: Overlay & Bar Age
panels.description: Time since the overlay cache and the newest ingested bar were last refreshed.

panels.title: Compute Cycle Errors
panels.description: Rate of errors raised during SMC overlay compute cycles (full + flow refresh).

panels.title: Feed Health Counters
panels.description: Feed-level counters: reconnects, Databento errors, unexpected errors, circuit breakers and partial restarts.

panels.title: Worker Liveness
panels.description: Per-worker thread liveness: live_feed, overlay_refresh, flow_refresh, ingest_processor.

panels.title: Feed Circuit Breakers (Total)
panels.description: Cumulative number of times the feed circuit breaker opened since process start.

panels.title: Feed Partial Restarts (Total)
panels.description: Cumulative number of partial feed restarts (lighter than a full reconnect).

panels.title: Success Rate (%)
panels.description: Percentage of recent SMC compute cycles that completed without errors.

panels.title: Market-open Request Health

targets.expr: (live_overlay_market_us_open{job="$job"} or live_overlay_market_us_open or vector(0)) + ((live_overlay_market_us_open{job="$job"} or live_overlay_market_us_open or vector(0)) * (((rate(live_overlay_smc_live_requests_total{job="$job"}[$__rate_interval]) or rate(live_overlay_smc_live_requests_total[$__rate_interval]) or vector(0)) > bool 0.001)))

panels.description: Synthetic signal that amplifies request health while the US market is open and suppresses it when closed.

panels.title: Uptime Resets (24h, est.)
panels.description: Estimated number of daemon restarts in the last 24 hours based on uptime resets.

panels.title: Restart Causes (24h, counted)
panels.description: Restart cause labels emitted by the daemon/deploy pipeline over the last 24 hours.

panels.title: Failure Mix
panels.description: Breakdown of compute/feed failures by category over time.

panels.title: Stale Budget Consumed (%)
panels.description: How much of the configured max_stale budget is currently consumed. 100 % means the overlay is at the stale threshold. This is derived from overlay_age_seconds / max_stale_seconds.

panels.title: Readiness Components Timeline
panels.description: Readiness components over time: feed healthy, workers healthy, overlay fresh, market open.

panels.title: Stale Responses (served)
panels.description: Count of health/status responses served while the overlay or feed data was stale.

panels.title: UptimeRobot Monitor States
panels.description: Per-monitor UptimeRobot state over time. paused = monitor intentionally paused; unknown = unrecognized status code.

panels.title: External Integrations
panels.description: Row grouping for external bridge health: UptimeRobot monitors and GitHub Actions workflows.

panels.title: UptimeRobot Bridge
panels.description: Whether the UptimeRobot API bridge is enabled and its last scrape succeeded.

panels.title: UptimeRobot Snapshot Age
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful UptimeRobot bridge poll.

panels.title: UptimeRobot Monitors
panels.description: Per-monitor up/down status and response time from UptimeRobot.

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Bridge
panels.description: Whether the GitHub workflow bridge is enabled and its last scrape succeeded.

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Snapshot Age
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful GitHub workflow bridge poll.

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Runs
panels.description: Rate of GitHub workflow runs by phase (runs per second).

panels.title: GitHub Workflow Status — Timeline
panels.description: Colour-coded latest status of each GitHub Actions workflow over time. Every row is one named workflow ({{workflow}} legend); the cell colour encodes the run conclusion — green=success, red=failure/timed-out, orange=cancelled/action-required, blue=in-progress, yellow=queued/stale, gray=unknown/skipped. Series come from live_overlay_github_workflow_phase_code{workflow,event,workflow_id}, sampled at the daemon's GitHub workflow bridge interval (so runs shorter than one scrape interval may not appear).

panels.title: Global Market Sessions
panels.description: Regular trading session state for US, Europe, and Asia markets.

panels.title: SLO & Reliability
panels.description: Row grouping for service-level objectives, no-data guards and error-budget burn rate.

panels.title: Latency vs. SLO (ms)
panels.description: p95 and p99 latency with a fixed 500 ms SLO threshold.

panels.title: Freshness SLO (Market Open, 1h)

targets.expr: 100 * ((sum(sum_over_time((((live_overlay_overlay_fresh{job="$job"} or live_overlay_overlay_fresh or vector(1)) * (live_overlay_market_us_open{job="$job"} or live_overlay_market_us_open or vector(0))))[1h:])) + (sum(sum_over_time((live_overlay_market_us_open{job="$job"} or live_overlay_market_us_open or vector(0))[1h:])) == bool 0)) / clamp_min(sum(sum_over_time((live_overlay_market_us_open{job="$job"} or live_overlay_market_us_open or vector(0))[1h:])), 1))

panels.description: Boolean SLO: overlay age under the freshness threshold while the market is open.

panels.title: No-Data Guard (Core Metrics)
panels.description: Fires when any of the core daemon metrics series disappears from Alloy.

panels.title: Error Budget Burn Rate (5m / 1h)
panels.description: Rate of bad events over 5m and 1h windows used for multi-window SLO alerting.

panels.title: Bridge Scrape Health Timeline
panels.description: External bridge scrape success over time (UptimeRobot + GitHub Workflows).

panels.title: Restart Causes (24h, by cause)
panels.description: Restart cause distribution over the last 24 hours.

panels.title: Hotspots — Symbols (Top)
panels.description: Most requested SMC overlay symbols by request count.

panels.title: News Provider State Codes
panels.description: Legend for News Provider panels directly below.

options.content: **News provider state codes:** `0` = unknown / no data, `1` = degraded, `2` = ok, `3` = disabled (not ingested). The **News Providers — Ingest Status & Reason** table shows the human-readable reason (e.g. API key missing, no active subscription, provider disabled) and whether each provider is actually consumed.

panels.title: Hotspots — Timeframes (Top)
panels.description: Most requested SMC timeframes by request count.

panels.title: Ingest Queue Backpressure
panels.description: Current ingest queue depth versus its configured maximum.

panels.title: Ingest Queue Lag (ms)
panels.description: End-to-end latency from bar arrival to ingestion completion.

panels.title: Provider Health
panels.description: Row grouping for live news provider health.

panels.title: News Provider Health State
panels.description: Aggregate news provider health flags (ok / degraded / unknown).

panels.title: News Snapshot Age & Provider Counts
panels.description: Age of the smc_live_news_snapshot.json provider state and aggregate provider counts (ok/degraded/unknown/disabled/consumed).

panels.title: News Providers — OK/Degraded (Drill-down)
panels.description: Per-provider ok/degraded/unknown state codes over time.

panels.title: News Providers — State Code
panels.description: Per-provider news state code: 0=unknown, 1=degraded, 2=ok, 3=disabled (not ingested).

panels.title: News Providers — Ingest Status & Reason
panels.description: Per-provider ingest status with the human-readable degraded reason (API key missing, no active subscription, provider disabled, …) and whether the provider is actually consumed.

panels.title: Active Alerts (live_overlay)
panels.description: Currently firing Grafana alerts from the live-overlay alert rule group.

panels.title: GitHub Workflows — Latest Run Detail
panels.description: Per-workflow detail of the most recent run the daemon observed: trigger event (schedule / push / …), current status, run duration and age. One row per named workflow. Driven by the labelled live_overlay_github_workflow_* gauges (phase_code, latest_duration_seconds, latest_age_seconds, latest_success).

panels.title: Active Trading Signals
panels.description: Number of currently active realtime breakout signals (A0 pre-breakout + A1 confirmed) the engine snapshot is reporting.

panels.title: A0 / A1 Breakouts
panels.description: Split of active signals by level: A0 = pre-breakout setup, A1 = confirmed breakout.

panels.title: Signals Snapshot Age
panels.description: Seconds since the realtime engine last wrote latest_realtime_signals.json. Red once the snapshot is older than the 480s (8 min) max signal age — stale signals should not be trusted. Shows N/A when the snapshot timestamp is unknown.

panels.title: Watched Symbols
panels.description: How many symbols the realtime engine is actively polling for breakout signals.

panels.title: Top Trading Signals — Latest Detail
panels.description: Per-symbol detail of the active breakout signals (top 50 by ranking score). Level A0 = pre-breakout, A1 = confirmed breakout; Direction is the trade bias; Score is the engine's v2 ranking; Tech is the 0–1 technical score; Change % is the intraday move; Freshness decays 1→0 as the signal ages. Driven by the labelled live_overlay_trading_signal_* gauges merged by symbol/level/direction/tier.

panels.title: Signal Score — Active Symbols
panels.description: Ranking score over time for each active breakout signal, labelled by symbol / level / direction. Lets you see which symbols are strengthening or fading.

panels.title: Daily Experiment — Snapshot Age
panels.description: Age of the latest Plan 2.8 family rollup (scripts/plan_2_8_tf_family_rollup.py). The rolling benchmark runs ~daily; green < 24h, yellow < 36h, red beyond means a run was skipped. N/A when the run date cannot be derived.

panels.title: Daily Experiment — Files Scanned
panels.description: Number of scoring files folded into the latest daily rollup. A sudden drop signals an upstream scoring/coverage problem.

panels.title: FVG 5m Verdict (Phase E2)
panels.description: Latest Phase E2 verdict for the FVG-time-to-fill 5m hypothesis vs baseline. measured = conclusive signal; underpowered = effect seen but not enough samples; insufficient/missing = not enough data yet.

panels.title: BOS 4H Verdict (Phase E2)
panels.description: Latest Phase E2 verdict for the BOS 4H stability hypothesis vs baseline.

panels.title: Daily Experiment — Latest Per-Family Detail
panels.description: Current-day hit-rate and sample size for each SMC setup family (BOS / OB / FVG / SWEEP) per timeframe, from the latest rollup. Hit-rate is colour-graded; n_events is the sample size behind it.

panels.title: Family Hit-Rate Over Time (accumulates daily)
panels.description: Live forward timeline: each daily benchmark run updates these gauges, so Prometheus records one step per day. Use the time picker (e.g. last 30 days) to see how each family/timeframe hit-rate trends going forward.

panels.title: Per-Day Family Hit-Rate — History (backfilled)
panels.description: Retained per-day history from plan_2_8_history.jsonl: exact hit-rate and sample size for each run date / timeframe / family. Backfills the daily statistics immediately (no need to wait for Prometheus to accumulate). Sort by Run Date to read the day-by-day timeline.

panels.title: TradingView Credential Age
panels.description: Hours since the TradingView Playwright storage-state login (meta.authValidatedAt) was last validated, from the daily credential-health report (tv_storage_state_age probe). Policy TTL is 72h: green < 57.6h (80% warn band), yellow 57.6–72h, red once the cached login is older than 72h and must be re-captured. N/A when no report/age is available.

panels.title: TradingView Credential Status
panels.description: Whether the latest credential-health report considers the TradingView storage-state login valid (severity not error). 1 = valid (cached login still within the 72h TTL), 0 = expired/error → re-capture TV_STORAGE_STATE. N/A when no report has been ingested yet.

panels.title: Container Resources (Railway)
panels.description: Row grouping for Railway container-level CPU, memory, disk and network metrics.

panels.title: CPU Usage (rate 5m)
panels.description: Container CPU usage in cores (rate over 5m) for live_overlay and signals_producer.

panels.title: Memory RSS (MB)
panels.description: Container resident memory in MB for live_overlay and signals_producer.

panels.title: Open File Descriptors
panels.description: Open file descriptor count per Railway service.

panels.title: Signals Producer Uptime
panels.description: Process uptime of the signals producer service.

panels.title: Live Overlay Uptime
panels.description: Process uptime of the live overlay daemon service.

panels.title: Python GC Collections (rate/5m)
panels.description: Python garbage collection frequency by generation.

panels.title: Metric Collector (Alloy)
panels.description: Row grouping for the Grafana Alloy sidecar scraping /metrics.

panels.title: Alloy CPU Usage (rate/5m)
panels.description: CPU usage of the Grafana Alloy collector.

panels.title: Alloy Memory RSS (MB)
panels.description: Resident memory of the Grafana Alloy collector.

panels.title: Alloy Uptime
panels.description: Process uptime of the Grafana Alloy collector.

panels.title: Alloy Go Goroutines
panels.description: Number of Go goroutines in the Grafana Alloy collector.

panels.title: Alloy Scrape Duration (s)
panels.description: Duration of Alloy scrape jobs against the daemon /metrics endpoint.

title: SMC Live Overlay Daemon
description: Live-overlay daemon operational dashboard: feed health, worker liveness, SMC overlay freshness, external bridge status, provider health, daily experiment metrics, and Railway container resources.
